title: Fix StripPrefix and StripPrefixRegex to slice the prefix using encoded prefix length
body:
### What does this PR do?

`StripPrefixRegex` matches the regex against the decoded `URL.Path` to extract the prefix, then uses `len(prefix)` to strip it from the encoded `URL.RawPath`. This could produce a corrupted RawPath when the prefix region contains percent-encoded characters.

This PR fixes the offset by scanning RawPath and advancing 3 bytes per %XX sequence, so the slice point correctly matches the encoded length of the prefix.

This PR also addresses the similar problem with `StripPrefix` middleware.


### Motivation

To avoid corrupted RawPath when the prefix contains encoded characters.


### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Co-authored-by: Mathis Urien <contact.lbf38@gmail.com>
author: gndz07

title: Fix allow colons and tildes in api.basePath validation
body:
### What does this PR do?

The basePath validation regex introduced in #12729 blocks `:` characters,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     

which breaks Rancher UI integration that uses paths like `/k8s/clusters/c-abcd0/api/v1/namespaces/my-ns/services/http:traefik:8080/proxy/`.

This updates the regex to also allow `:` and `~`, which are valid URI `pchar` characters per RFC 3986 and pose no XSS risk (the switch to `html/template` in #12729 already handles escaping).

### Motivation

Fixes #12836

### More

title: Fix auth-response-headers whitespace trimming in ingress-nginx provider
body:
### What does this PR do?

Trim whitespace from auth-response-headers annotation values when splitting on commas. Previously, "X-Foo, Authorization" produced ["X-Foo", " Authorization"] (with leading space), causing the header to never match. This aligns with how other comma-separated annotations are already handled in annotations.go.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
### Motivation

Fixes #12848

title: Fix postgres STARTTLS with TLS termination
body:
### What does this PR do?

The merge of v2.11 into v3.6 (b1b520b18) changed `clientHelloInfo` from a `Peek`-based approach to a `TeeReader`+`readOnlyConn` approach. In `servePostgres()`, the TeeReader was set up **before** consuming the SSLRequest bytes, causing them to be included in the peeked data passed to the TLS handler.

The TLS handshake then failed with `"tls: first record does not look like a TLS handshake"` because it read the SSLRequest bytes (starting with `0x00`) instead of the TLS ClientHello (`0x16`).
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
The fix introduces a new conn to type to centralize Peek and Read operations.

### Motivation

Fixes #12842 

### More

- [X] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

title: Preserve health check status updater when service has middlewares
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

After applying the middleware chain, wrap the resulting handler in a `statusUpdaterHandler` that preserves the `StatusUpdater` interface from the original load balancer.                                                                                                                                                 

### Motivation

When a service has both service-level middlewares and health checks configured, the middleware chain wrapping was losing the `StatusUpdater` interface, preventing health check status propagation to the parent load balancer.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] ~Added/updated documentation~

### Additional Notes

Targeting v3.7 since it was introduced in v3.7.0-ea.1
author: jspdown

Preserve health check status updater when service has middlewares

When a service has both service-level middlewares and health checks
configured, the middleware chain wrapping was losing the StatusUpdater
interface, preventing health check status propagation to the parent
load balancer.

title: Add vulnerability submission quality guidelines
body:
## What does this PR do?

- Add a "Submission Quality Guidelines" section to the security documentation
- Require contributors to test and validate vulnerabilities with a working proof of concept before submitting
- State that unvalidated AI/LLM-generated reports will be closed immediately
- Warn that repeat offenders may have their accounts blocked

## Motivation
Addresses the growing number of low-quality security advisories submitted without proper human validation.
author: emilevauge

commits.message:
Add security vulnerability submission quality guidelines

Address the growing number of low-quality, AI-generated security
reports by documenting clear expectations: contributors must test
and validate vulnerabilities before submission, and unvalidated
AI/LLM-generated reports will be closed immediately. Repeat
offenders may have their accounts blocked.

title: Add OTel-conformant trace context attributes to access logs
body:
### What does this PR do?

Emit OTel-conformant `trace_id` and `span_id` log attributes alongside the existing `TraceId`/`SpanId` fields in access logs.

This is a non-breaking change: existing parsers relying on `TraceId`/`SpanId` continue to work, while OTel-compatible observability tools (Grafana, Jaeger, etc.) can now automatically correlate logs with traces.

### Motivation

The [OTel Logging Trace Context specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/compatibility/logging_trace_context/) requires `trace_id` and `span_id` (snake_case) as log attribute names for automatic log/trace correlation. Traefik currently only emits PascalCase names (`TraceId`, `SpanId`), which prevents automatic correlation in OTel-based tooling.

Closes #12798

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Both the legacy (`TraceId`, `SpanId`) and the new OTel-conformant (`trace_id`, `span_id`) attributes carry the same values. This dual-emit approach ensures backward compatibility while enabling OTel tooling out of the box.
author: mmatur

commits.message: Fix follow otel semconv for access log

title: Fix unnecessary escaping of pipe in regexp examples
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
Removes unnecessary backslash escaping of the pipe character (`|`) in regexp examples in the HTTP routing rules & priority documentation. `\|` matches a literal pipe in Go regexp, while `|` is the alternation operator, which is what the examples intend to demonstrate.

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
The regexp examples in the [rules-and-priority docs](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/http/routing/rules-and-priority/) escape the pipe as `\|` inside inline code blocks in Markdown tables.

### More

- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->
None.

body:
### What does this PR do?

This PR brings a fix for `use-regex` annotation for the ingress-nginx provider. When this annotation is set, it should apply to all ingresses on the same host. Additionally, location modifier regex should be case insensitive.

This PR also brings `strictValidatePathType` provider configuration, which allows user to configure whether or not they want to reject ingress paths containing regex characters when pathType is Prefix or Exact.

Fixes #12560 

### Motivation

To match the behavior of ingress-nginx when applying `use-regex` annotation.

title: Bump sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api to v1.5.1
body:
### What does this PR do?

Bump `sigs.k8s.io/gateway-api` from v1.4.0 to v1.5.1, promoting TLSRoute support from `v1alpha2` (experimental) to `v1` (stable GA).

**Key changes:**

- **TLSRoute promoted to GA**: TLSRoute no longer requires the `experimentalChannel` option. All TLSRoute types migrated from `gatev1alpha2` to `gatev1`.
- **TLS `Terminate` mode support**: Added `TLSRouteModeTerminate` as a supported extended feature, enabling TLS termination on TLS protocol listeners (previously only Passthrough was supported).
- **Deny unknown TLS hosts**: Added a default low-priority catch-all TCP router (`deny-unknown-host`) that rejects TLS connections for undefined hostnames, preventing traffic leaking to unintended backends.
- **Mixed TLS mode conflict detection**: New validation rejects listeners that mix Passthrough and Terminate modes on the same port, setting `ProtocolConflict` status conditions.
- **Wildcard hostname matching fix**: Fixed `findMatchingHostname` to correctly return the more specific hostname when both listener and route use wildcards (e.g., `*.bar.foo.com` is now correctly matched against `*.foo.com`).
- **HostSNIRegexp fix**: Corrected the wildcard-to-regex conversion to use `[a-z0-9-]+` instead of `[a-z0-9-\.]+`, preventing wildcard labels from matching across dot boundaries.
- **BackendTLSPolicy hardening**: Added empty string validation for `ca.crt` in ConfigMap-based CA certificates.
- **TLS protocol decoupled from TCPRoute**: TLS listeners now only support TLSRoute (no longer TCPRoute), aligning with the upstream spec.
- Updated CRD manifests, conformance test fixtures, and documentation for v1.5.1.

### Motivation

Align with the latest Gateway API specification. TLSRoute has been promoted to GA in v1.5.0, and v1.5.1 includes bugfixes to the conformance test suite.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

#### Conformance Test Results (v1.5.1)

| Profile | Core | Extended | Result |
|---------|------|----------|--------|
| **GATEWAY-HTTP** | 33/33 | 20/20 | All passed |
| **GATEWAY-GRPC** | 13/13 | — | All passed |
| **GATEWAY-TLS** | 18/18 | 3/3 (`TLSRouteModeTerminate`) | All passed |

title: Implement proxy-http-version annotation
body:
## Description
Implements `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-http-version` annotation for the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider.

Related to #12631

## What does this PR do?
- Adds support for controlling HTTP protocol version used for backend communication
- Implicitly creates a ServersTransport with `DisableHTTP2=true` when annotation is set to "1.1"
- Logs warning for unsupported value "1.0" (Traefik doesn't support forcing HTTP/1.0)

## Implementation Details
- Added `ProxyHTTPVersion` field to `ingressConfig` struct
- Modified `buildServersTransport()` to set `DisableHTTP2=true` when annotation value is "1.1"

## Supported Values
- `"1.1"` - Forces HTTP/1.1 by disabling HTTP/2 ✅
- `"1.0"` - Not supported (logs warning) ⚠️

## Testing
- Added test fixture for valid case ("1.1")
- Added test fixture for unsupported case ("1.0")
- Unit tests verify `DisableHTTP2` is set correctly
- All local verifications pass

## Documentation
- Updated provider documentation with annotation details
- Documented limitation regarding HTTP/1.0 support
author: KshitijBharde

commits.message:
feat(provider/k8s-ingress-nginx): implement proxy-http-version annotation

Implements nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-http-version annotation
to control HTTP protocol version for backend communication.

- Supported value: '1.1' (sets DisableHTTP2=true)
- Unsupported value: '1.0' (logs warning, uses default)
- Implicitly creates ServersTransport with DisableHTTP2=true
- Explicit ServersTransport configuration takes precedence

docs(k8s-ingress-nginx): document proxy-http-version annotation

Add documentation for nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-http-version
annotation support in the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider reference.

- Add new "Backend Protocol" section with proxy-http-version annotation
- Document supported value "1.1" and unsupported value "1.0"
- Remove annotation from unsupported list
- Clarify that "1.0" logs a warning but is not supported

title: Service failover support in TraefikService CRD
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

This PR adds `failover` support to the `TraefikService` CRD, alongside the existing `weighted`, `mirroring`, and `highestRandomWeight` service types.                               
                                                      
The failover mechanism already exists in the dynamic configuration but was not exposed through the Kubernetes CRD.

  ```yaml
  apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
  kind: TraefikService
  metadata:
    name: my-failover
  spec:
    failover:
      service:
        name: main-svc
        port: 80
      fallback:
        name: backup-svc
        port: 80
      errors:
        status:
          - "500-504"
        maxRequestBodyBytes: 1048576
```

### Motivation

Being able to configure failover service on Kubernetes.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### What does this PR do?

Replace the imported `text/template` with `html/template` for the dashboard template and add validation for the `basePath` configuration.


### Motivation

A stricter regex validation will allow user to be warned early if the URL in their `basePath` is not a valid URL.
`text/template` provides no automatic escaping for HTML or JavaScript contexts. Switching to `html/template` ensures values rendered into the dashboard template are properly escaped, preventing potential injection issues.

title: Allow entry points to be specified on Nginx Ingresses
body: ### What does this PR do? Fixes #12646 Users can now explicitly specify which entry points an NGINX compatible ingress get exposed on instead of exposing it on every single entry point. There's no reason for my web server to exposed on my mail ports. Now, users can specify a command line argument: ``` --providers.kubernetesingressnginx.tlsentrypoints=websecure --providers.kubernetesingressnginx.nontlsentrypoints=web ``` ### Motivation I'm migrating from ingress-nginx to Traefik and the KubernetesIngressNginx provider makes this a lot easier. However, I also use Traefik to proxy TCP, non-HTTP based traffic and I noticed that Traefik appeared to expose my web servers over those TCP endpoints: Now: ### More - [X] Added/updated tests - [X] Added/updated documentation ### Additional Notes My previous attempted used annotations here: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12691. A maintainer asked me to change to this style. I'm not actually sure if I did the names correctly. I reused the existing `NonTLSEntryPoints` and added a `TLSEntryPoints` provider flag. However, I also used the httpEntryPoints and httpsEntryPoints for JSON/TOML because that's what the maintainer in the issue suggested.

title: Fix Go version pinning in CI workflows
body:
### What does this PR do?

- Add a `.go-version` file to pin CI builds to a specific Go patch version (1.25.7)
- Switch all CI workflows from `go-version-file: 'go.mod'` to `go-version-file: '.go-version'`
- Revert `go.mod` to `go 1.25.0`, keeping it as the minimum required version

This decouples the CI Go version from the `go.mod` directive — `go.mod` declares the minimum language version the module needs, while `.go-version` controls exactly which toolchain CI uses.

### Motivation

Traefik is used as a library. (cf: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12649#discussion_r2841062608)

title: Support limit-rps annotation for Ingress NGINX
body:
## What does this PR do?

Completes part of "Implement more NGINX Ingress Annotations #12631".

Implemented the following NGINX Ingress annotation:
- **`nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/limit-rps`**: Maps to Traefik's RateLimit middleware.

*(Note: This PR was separated from the `server-alias` PR as requested. I'd be happy to coordinate with @Ph4rell on his `limit-rpm` implementation to ensure both flow consistently!)*

## Motivation
#12631

## More
- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

## Additional Notes
**Implementation Detail:**
- **Rate Limiting Algorithm**: Mapped `limit-rps` to Traefik's RateLimit middleware with a 1-second period. While NGINX uses a Leaky Bucket and Traefik uses a Token Bucket, a default burst multiplier of 5 is used to match NGINX's baseline behavior.
author: amazon7737

reviews.body:
Hello @amazon7737,

Thank you for opening the PR! I’ve left a small comment regarding the burst configuration. Additionally, as mentioned in this [comment](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/12631#issuecomment-3915361071), there may be differences between how Traefik’s rate limiting algorithm works and how Nginx handles rate limiting. It could be helpful to include a brief comparison and highlight any notable differences 🙂

commits.message:
feat(ingress-nginx): support limit-rps annotation

- Implement limit-rps annotation mapping to RateLimit middleware.
- Set default burst to 5x average for NGINX compatibility.
- Add documentation with algorithm comparison (Token Bucket vs Leaky Bucket).
- Add unit tests and fixtures.
- Resolved conflicts with latest master.

title: Support upstream-keepalive-timeout
body:
### What does this PR do?

This PR introduces support for upstream-keepalive-timeout configuration for the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider.

~This PR introduces support for the `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/upstream-keepalive-timeout` annotation within the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider.~

~It reads the integer value provided in the annotation (in seconds) and maps it directly to Traefik's internal `ServersTransport.ForwardingTimeouts.IdleConnTimeout` configuration for that specific route.~

### Motivation

Related to #12631

~With the impending retirement of the Kubernetes NGINX Ingress Controller, many users are migrating to Traefik. To ensure a seamless migration, it's crucial that they can maintain their existing routing configurations without rewriting their manifests.~

~Previously, Traefik ignored this specific annotation, which prevented users in high-traffic environments from properly tuning their upstream TCP keep-alive connection behavior. This PR bridges that gap.~

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

~- The annotation expects a standard integer value representing seconds, which precisely mirrors the native NGINX Ingress Controller behavior.~ 
~- A new YAML fixture and test case have been added to `kubernetes_test.go` to ensure this translation is strictly enforced and regressions are prevented.~
author: jcob-sikorski

commits.message:
feat: add upstream keepalive timeout

Users migrating from NGINX Ingress Controller to Traefik need to maintain
their existing routing configurations. Previously, Traefik did not
support the NGINX annotation for configuring the upstream keep-alive
timeout, preventing seamless migrations for users relying on this feature.

This commit introduces support for the
nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/upstream-keepalive-timeout annotation.
It parses the requested timeout value in seconds and maps it directly
to Traefik's internal IdleConnTimeout setting on the generated
ServersTransport.

 - Adds UpstreamKeepaliveTimeout to the ingressConfig struct
 - Updates buildServersTransport to apply the parsed duration
 - Includes unit tests and fixtures to verify the translation

Relates to: #12631

title: Support limit-rpm annotation for ingress-nginx
body:
## What does this PR do?

  Adds support for `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/limit-rpm` in the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider.

  When set on an Ingress, Traefik now creates a `rateLimit` middleware on the generated router with:
  - `average = <limit-rpm value>`
  - `period = 1m`
  - `burst = <limit-rpm> * 5 (default limit-burst-multiplier value)`

  Invalid or non-positive values are ignored.

  ## Why is this change needed?

  `limit-rpm` is a common ingress-nginx annotation used during migration.
  Supporting it reduces migration friction and aligns behavior with existing annotation-compatibility goals.

  ## How was it implemented?

  - Added `LimitRPM *int` to parsed ingress annotations.
  - Added middleware wiring in router middleware assembly:
    - `applyLimitRPMConfiguration(...)`
  - Added tests:
    - Annotation parsing coverage
    - Focused middleware behavior coverage
  - Updated docs:
    - Added `limit-rpm` to supported annotations
    - Removed `limit-rpm` from unsupported annotations

  ## Files changed

  - `pkg/provider/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/annotations.go`
  - `pkg/provider/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/kubernetes.go`
  - `pkg/provider/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/annotations_test.go`
  - `docs/content/reference/routing-configuration/kubernetes/ingress-nginx.md`

  - This maps to Traefik's per-source rate limiter semantics via `rateLimit`.
  - Current implementation sets:
    - `Average: limit-rpm`
    - `Period: 1m`
    - `Burst: limit-rpm * 5 (default burst multiplier)` 

  ## Checklist

  - [x] Solves one problem only (`limit-rpm` support)
  - [x] Small, focused PR
  - [x] Tests added for new behavior
  - [x] Documentation updated
  - [x] No `go.mod` dependency changes
  - [ ] Validation checks pass
  - [ ] All tests pass

title: Fix TLS handshake error handling
body:
### What does this PR do?

Stops execution immediately when a TLS handshake read error occurs, instead of continuing the flo

### Motivation
Currently, when a read error occurs during the TLS handshake, the code attempts a second handshake. Since connection parameters (such as read timeout) differ between the two attempts, some errors end up being silently ignored. This change ensures errors are properly surfaced on the first failure.

title: Fix HasSecureHeadersDefined returning false when stsSeconds is 0
body:
Fixes #11839

When configuring `stsSeconds=0` as the only header middleware option, traefik rejects the config with `"headers configuration not valid"`. This happens because `HasSecureHeadersDefined()` checks `h.STSSeconds != 0`, which is false for zero — making the middleware think nothing was configured.

The root cause is that `STSSeconds` is `int64`, so we can't distinguish between "not set" and "explicitly set to 0".

**Fix**: Change `STSSeconds` from `int64` to `*int64`. Now `nil` means "not configured" and `0` means "user explicitly set it to zero". The check in `HasSecureHeadersDefined()` becomes `h.STSSeconds != nil`.

Steps to reproduce:
```yaml
http:
  middlewares:
    no-hsts:
      headers:
        stsSeconds: 0
```

Before this fix: `error="headers configuration not valid"`
After this fix: middleware is created successfully

Changes:
- `pkg/config/dynamic/middlewares.go`: field type `int64` -> `*int64`, check `!= 0` -> `!= nil`
- `pkg/config/dynamic/zz_generated.deepcopy.go`: handle pointer deep copy
- `pkg/middlewares/headers/secure.go`: dereference pointer when passing to `unrolled/secure`
- Tests added in `middleware_test.go` and `headers_test.go` covering the stsSeconds=0 case
- Existing tests updated to use pointer values
author: veeceey

commits.message:
Fix HasSecureHeadersDefined returning false when stsSeconds is 0

Change STSSeconds field type from int64 to *int64 to distinguish
between "not set" (nil) and "explicitly set to 0". Previously,
configuring only stsSeconds=0 caused "headers configuration not valid"
error because HasSecureHeadersDefined() treated the zero value as
unset.

Fixes #11839

### What does this PR do?

The documentation for label selector configuration often uses all lowercase (`labelSelector`). This PR updates the documentation to consistently refer to the setting as `labelSelector`.

### Motivation

While casing doesn't appear to matter for configuration passed via the CLI, the documentation doesn't align with how other configuration sources ([Helm](https://github.com/traefik/traefik-helm-chart/blob/master/traefik/values.yaml#L300), [YAML](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/master/docs/content/reference/static-configuration/file.yaml#L157), [TOML](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/master/docs/content/reference/static-configuration/file.toml#L142)) expect to be provided the value.


### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

1.  As is, this MR **breaks existing external direct links** to this configuration option, such as in the helm repo, forum posts, etc: 

    https://github.com/traefik/traefik-helm-chart/blob/master/traefik/values.yaml#L299

    I tried to keep the existing href/id for the anchor tags, but it was failing CI since `make generate` wants to keep them consistent with the text value. If this is a dealbreaker for this PR, I completely understand.

2. I didn't edit [configuration-options.md](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/master/docs/content/reference/install-configuration/configuration-options.md) since its claims to be code-generated, but I couldn't find how to re-generate it.

title: Fix incorrect hostname matching between listener and route
body:
### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
Fix `findMatchingHostnames` returning the wrong hostname specificity during comparison.
That behavior is fixed by this PR.

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
If a listener hostname is `*.foo.com`, and the HTTPRoute hostname is `*.bar.foo.com`, the returned domain would be `*.foo.com`, causing all requests matching `*.foo.com` to be routed to the service that specifically advertised `*.bar.foo.com`.

This is because, when comparing the hostnames, the returned hostname is determined by the number of wildcards in each domain. The one with the fewer wildcards is picked, and if both hostnames have the same number of wildcards, the listener is picked.
This has been changed to just return the second hostname (HTTPRoute) as previous checks have already verified that it is has higher specificity.

I've also added a test checking this behavior.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

I considered opening an issue, but the fix for this is so small that I thought I'd open a PR, hope that's okay! I'm also not a Go developer, but the logic change is quite simple.
author: TheColorman

reviews.body:
Hi @TheColorman,

> This seems awfully similar to your successful test case. Is there a different version of Traefik/the chart I should be trying this with?

We figured out that there was a mistake on our end while testing, and your test is indeed failing currently. The changes you are proposing are indeed fixing it, we agree with it, and we even need it for the update to Kubernetes Gateway v1.5.1: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12768.

Thanks again for this contribution and for your time!

title: Make TLSStore gracefully handle missing secrets
body:
### What does this PR do?

Currently, when loading certificates from TlsStore if there is an issue reading one of the secrets the whole TlsStore is not loaded.

It might be possible that you configure the TlsStore with a secret that does not already exist (i.e. you provision a certificate with cert-manager that is new, and it takes a while to provision or fails to do so).

This pull request allows the TlsStore to work even if there is an issue loading one of the certificates.

Fixes #12523

### Motivation

It's easy to overlook this existing implementation detail, preventing all your certificates from being loaded. I'd rather classify this as a bug, but it is subject to interpretation on how this is currently built and the potential impact of using broken or not yet available secrets in TlsStore.

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->
author: barhun

reviews.body:
Thanks for the contribution @barhun! This PR correctly addresses the feedback from the previous PR by using `FileOrContent` on the existing `token` field.

## Review Summary

### What works well

1. **Follows established patterns** - This matches how `Token` fields are implemented in Kubernetes providers (e.g., `pkg/provider/kubernetes/gateway/kubernetes.go:67`)
2. **Proper token reading** - Correctly calls `config.Token.Read()` to handle both direct content and file paths
3. **Handles trailing newlines** - Using `strings.TrimSpace()` is important for file-based secrets that often have trailing newlines
4. **Security preserved** - The `loggable:"false"` tag is maintained

### Requested changes

1. **Documentation description** - Please update the field description to match other `FileOrContent` Token fields:
   ```go
   Token tTypes.FileOrContent `description:"InfluxDB v2 access token. It accepts either a token value or a file path to the token." ...`
   ```

2. **Documentation** - The checkbox indicates documentation wasn't updated. The docs at `docs/content/observability/metrics/influxdb2.md` should mention that the token option now accepts either a direct value or a file path.

### Optional improvements

3. **Tests** - While the existing test still passes (string literals are compatible with `FileOrContent`), consider adding a test case that reads a token from a temporary file to validate the file-reading path.

---

The implementation itself is correct and minimal. Once the description and documentation are updated, this should be good to merge.

LGTM
You just need to run the `go generate` command in order for the CI to pass.

title: Add CertificateTimeout ACME configuration option
body:
### What does this PR do?

This feature allows each configured `certificateResolver` to specify a finalization (sometimes referred to as certificate) timeout. The configured value is passed to the Lego ACME client library and is used to determine how long the the client waits for an order to transition from ready -> valid.

```yaml
certificatesResolvers:
  prod:
    acme:
      email: certs@acme.com
      certificateTimeout: "2m"
```

### Motivation

The default Lego ACME client timeout is 30 seconds. This is too short for some certificate authorities and can cause intermittent timeouts. Traefik already passes through several configuration values to the Lego ACME client so it seemed like an easy fix with code patterns already available.

### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

This type of problem is present in most ACME clients that develop towards LetsEncrypt as a baseline. I created this change both to allow our Traefik instances to successfully request certificates and to contribute back a longstanding community request.

I did not include tests because it's inherently difficult to write a timeout test. It usually slows down the suite and tests for config passing seem rote instead of useful. I am willing to write tests if necessary.

I am willing to update documentation if this request gains traction. I'd like to agree on a configuration key name first.
author: ceko

Thanks for your contribution @ceko 

Could you also add documentation?

title: Multi-layer routing
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.5

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.5

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

This PR introduces multi-layer routing, a new hierarchical routing capability that enables parent routers to apply middleware before child routers make final routing decisions. 

This allows progressive request enrichment where each routing layer can add context (headers, authentication, etc.) that influences subsequent routing decisions.

Router tree parsing is performed once during configuration loading
No performance impact on existing routing (single-layer routers)
Child routers are built lazily as part of parent router muxer

This is a new opt-in feature activated only when `parentRefs` is configured.

### Motivation

Multi-layer routing addresses advanced routing scenarios that require progressive request enrichment, where a routing layer adds context, enabling increasingly specific routing decisions.

For example, authentication-based routing: parent router authenticates and adds user context headers, child routers route based on roles/permissions.

Fixes: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/5098

### Technical changes

RouterManager (pkg/server/router/router.go):
- ParseRouterTree(): Builds parent-child relationships, validates configuration, detects cycles/orphans
- buildChildRoutersMuxer(): Creates muxer for child routers
- Router tree traversal with cycle detection and reachability analysis

Kubernetes CRD provider (pkg/provider/kubernetes/crd/):
- New ParentRefs field in IngressRoute spec
- resolveParentRouterNames(): Resolves parent IngressRoute references to router names
- Cross-namespace parent reference support with validation

Access logs (pkg/middlewares/accesslog/):
- ConcatFieldHandler: Concatenates router names instead of overwriting (shows full routing path)

Dynamic configuration (pkg/config/dynamic/):
- Add `ParentRefs` field to Router struct
- Add `ChildRefs` field to RouterInfo struct
- Runtime validation for router hierarchy constraints

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Co-authored-by: Romain <rtribotte@users.noreply.github.com>

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

title: Simplify Go version refs in CI templates
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
To ever so slightly simplify the amount of bumps and changes needed to update the Go version, this PR proposes to use the `go-version-file` functionality of the `setup-go` GitHub action, which allows extracting the version from a specified file. Consequently this means that only the `go.mod` file in this project contains the (single source of truth) Go version.
See also https://github.com/actions/setup-go?tab=readme-ov-file#go-version-file.

It also ensures that the `check-latest: true` flag is applied consistently; if this PR would not make it entirely, this should probably be included as individual fix, to ensure all jobs always run on latest patch version rather than the (latest) cached version.

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
Simplifying Go version bump process.

### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->
If a need ever arises, the `go-version` identifier can still be set on individual jobs, taking precedence over the version file directive. 

Also there are no longer any explicit Go version mentioned in CI job step names, but this should be clear enough anyways.
author: jnoordsij

commits.message: Do not specify patch version in go.mod
Extract Go version from go.mod in CI jobs
Consistently use check-latest flag on setup-go action

title: Cap TLS record length to RFC 8446 limit in ClientHello peeking
body:
### What does this PR do?

- Validate recLen against the RFC 8446 Section 5.1 maximum (16,384 bytes) before allocating the bufio.Reader in clientHelloInfo
- Reject TLS records advertising a payload length exceeding this limit, preventing oversized per-connection buffer allocations

### Motivation

A malicious client can send a crafted TLS record header with an inflated length field (up to 0xFFFF) and then stall, forcing Traefik to allocate an oversized buffer (~65 KiB instead of ~4 KiB) per connection. With enough concurrent connections against a memory-constrained instance, this can cause
  resource strain. Capping at the RFC-mandated bound eliminates this amplification vector.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- ~[ ] Added/updated documentation~

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

title: Clarify SNI selection
body:
### What does this PR do?

- The PR adds some enhancements to the existing docs. It clarifies and elaborates on how Traefik selects user-defined TLS certificates during the TLS handshake (SNI-based selection).
- The PR intends to documents the default-certificate fallback behavior when SNI is missing or no certificate matches.
- The PR provides a local development example using `mkcert` (along with YAML + TOML dynamic configuration).
- The PR also adds links for further reference for end-to-end “Enable TLS” examples for Docker, Swarm, and Kubernetes.


### Motivation

Issue #8073 highlighted a recurring confusion around `tls.certificates`, whether it automatically “matches by domain” and why the default certificate is sometimes served.

While the current exiting docs already cover the individual pieces, I felt that the readers of the TLS Certificates reference page would benefit from a detailed explanation of SNI-based selection plus a practical local-dev workflow (`mkcert`) and pointers to complete provider examples.

Fixes #8073

### AI Usage 
Used AI to evaluate if the PR changes correctly fits the scope and fits well with language and overall style of the page. Used to make sure if all required points are covered.

title: Use ParentBased sampler to respect parent span sampling decision
body:
### What does this PR do?

Changes the OpenTelemetry tracer sampler from `TraceIDRatioBased` to `ParentBased(TraceIDRatioBased)` to respect incoming parent span's sampling flags.

### Motivation

Currently, Traefik ignores the parent span's sampling decision. If an upstream service marks a trace as non-sampled (`traceparent` flags=00), Traefik will still sample the request based solely on `sampleRate`. This breaks distributed tracing consistency across service meshes.


### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

- In https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/11334 it was suggested implementing the functionality via a plugin. However, since the decision on sampling is made before plugins are executed, changes to the traefik code are required.
author: xe-leon

title: Add routing configuration extension points
body:
### What does this PR do?

This PR adds the ability to extend dynamic configuration structures with custom fields.

To achieve this, it introduces a separate Go module (pkg/config/dynamic/ext) containing empty placeholder structs. These structs are embedded into configuration types like HTTPConfiguration and Router. To extend these types, you can replace the module with your own implementation using a Go module replace directive.

Adding extensibility to the configuration types also required fixing how configurations get merged. The existing merge code was scattered across providers with a lot of duplication, and it could not handle custom fields. The provider.Merge function has been rewritten to use reflection to walk configuration structs, which allows it to support embedded fields.

A merge strategy concept has been introduced to handle different provider needs: one strategy merges compatible services (used by the aggregator), and another skips duplicates (used by the file provider).

### Motivation

Traefik Hub needs to attach additional metadata to routers and other resources.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] ~~Added/updated documentation~~

title: Add configuration transformer mechanism to the ConfigurationWatcher
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

Adds a configuration transformer mechanism to the ConfigurationWatcher that allows modifying dynamic configurations before they are applied. Transformers receive the triggering provider name and the full set of configurations from all providers.


### Motivation

This mechanism complements #12562  (dynamic config extension) by providing a way to compute and populate extended dynamic configuration fields. While #12562 allows extending configuration structures with custom fields, this PR provides the hook to compute and populate those fields based on provider configurations.

This enables dynamic configuration transformations from external components without requiring changes to individual providers.

title: Add support for auth-signin annotation
body:
This PR adds support for the nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/auth-signin annotation to Traefik's NGINX Ingress provider, addressing a critical migration blocker documented in issue #12490.

**Problem Statement**
Users migrating from NGINX Ingress to Traefik face incompatibility issues with OAuth2-protected services. The auth-signin annotation is essential for OAuth2 redirect flows but was previously unsupported in Traefik's NGINX Ingress provider. Notably, this annotation was previously documented as unsupported in Traefik.

**Solution**
The PR implements support for the auth-signin annotation, which specifies the URL where users should be redirected for authentication when they attempt to access a protected resource without valid credentials.
author: DesalLama

reviews.body:
Hi @DesalLama 

Thanks for your contribution

After analyzing the nginx ingress controller behavior and comparing it with this PR's implementation, I've identified that the current approach needs to be revised to match nginx's actual behavior.

## Current Implementation Issue

The current PR passes the signin URL via an `X-Auth-Signin` header to the auth service, expecting the auth service to handle the redirect. However, **this doesn't match how nginx ingress works**:

- **nginx behavior**: Intercepts 401 responses from the auth service and performs a 302 redirect to the signin URL directly
- **Current PR behavior**: Passes header to auth service, which standard auth services like oauth2-proxy don't use

## Proposed Solution

Extend the ForwardAuth middleware to natively support 401→redirect behavior:

### 1. Add `AuthSigninURL` field to ForwardAuth config (`pkg/config/dynamic/middlewares.go`)

### 2. Modify ForwardAuth middleware (`pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go`)
- Add `buildSigninURL()` helper for nginx variable substitution
- Intercept 401 responses and redirect when `AuthSigninURL` is configured:

### 3. Update nginx ingress provider (`pkg/provider/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/kubernetes.go`)
- Simplify to use `ForwardAuth.AuthSigninURL` instead of creating separate headers middleware
- Remove the `X-Auth-Signin` header injection approach

## Variable Substitution Support

| Variable | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `$scheme` | Request scheme (checks TLS, then X-Forwarded-Proto) | `https` |
| `$host` | Request host header | `app.example.com` |
| `$request_uri` | Full URI with query string | `/protected?foo=bar` |
| `$escaped_request_uri` | URL-encoded URI | `%2Fprotected%3Ffoo%3Dbar` |

## Test Coverage Needed

- `TestForwardAuthSigninRedirectOn401` - 401 with AuthSigninURL → 302 redirect
- `TestForwardAuthSigninRedirectWithVariables` - variable substitution
- `TestForwardAuthNoRedirectOn401WithoutSigninURL` - 401 without config →

title: Add authSignInURL in forward auth middleware
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
Fixes #12171

This PR introduces a new option `unauthenticated_address` for the `forwardAuth` middleware.  
When the authentication endpoint returns an unauthenticated error response,
Traefik forwards the request to this address and returns its response to the client.  
This removes the need for configuring a separate error-handling middleware in common authentication redirect setups.

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
Using the forwardAuth middleware currently requires users to configure an additional `errors` middleware to handle unauthenticated responses and redirect users to an authentication flow.  

By adding the `unauthenticated_address` field, we provide a simpler and more intuitive configuration path while keeping backward compatibility.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

title: Alter TLS renewal period
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

This PR modifies the logic that determines when certificates should be renewed. Focus has been given on the Let's Encrypt `shortlived` profile as well as the changes coming in the next few years where LE will issue certificates at 45 days (and all issuers will required to issue certificates valid for 47 days at most by March 15, 2029).


### Motivation

Fixes https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/12461


### More

- [X] Added/updated tests
- [X] Added/updated documentation
author: LtHummus

reviews.body: Overall this change looks good. However there is a table in `docs/content/https/acme.md` (paragraph about `certificatesDuration`) that should be updated, too.

Hello @LtHummus,

Thanks for this contribution!

After discussions, we think the renew period should be adjusted to   something more close the 30% recommended by LE, and adjust the renew interval to have around 20 tries.
Could you please adapt the PR in that direction?

commits.message: alter renewal periods to make more sense for letsencrypt changes
remove unneeded newline in imports; fix acme duration table in docs
fix name of Let's Encrypt in test name
reduce interval to 3 hours for 7 day certs
change shortlived renewal to be closer to 30% of duration

title: Document negative priority support for routers
body:
Currently there is no official documentation which either confirms or denies that negative router priorities are supported; the only information about this that can be found online seems to be outdated (reflecting Traefik 1.0).

Negative priority values do seem to actually be supported since Traefik v2.0.  A router with a negative priority will be evaluated after all routers with positive or default priorities, which is useful for defining fallback or catch-all routes, so let's document this explicitly.

<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
Clarify the documentation about negative priorities.

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
We wanted to add a negative priority route and weren't sure if it would work or not.

### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [X] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->
author: understood-the-assignment

reviews.body:
Hello @understood-the-assignment,

Thanks for this contribution!

I think the document should be kept simple and factual. Although negative values are supported, they are not necessary. A router that should be evaluated last can be safely configured with the lowest positive value, 1.

commits.message:
Document negative priority support for routers

Currently there is no official documentation which either confirms or
denies that negative router priorities are supported; the only
information about this that can be found online seems to be
outdated (reflecting Traefik 1.0).

Negative priority values do seem to actually be supported since
Traefik v2.0.  A router with a negative priority will be evaluated
after all routers with positive or default priorities, which is useful
for defining fallback or catch-all routes, so let's document this
explicitly.

body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?



strings.SplitSeq (introduced in Go 1.23)  returns a lazy sequence (strings.Seq), allowing gopher to iterate over tokens one by one without creating an intermediate slice.

It significantly reduces memory allocations and can improve performance for long strings.

More info: https://github.com/golang/go/issues/61901



### Motivation

title: Fix health check ping
body: ### What does this PR do? Fixes healthcheck command failure when `http_proxy` environment variable is set in the Traefik container. ### Motivation Fixes: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/12455 When `http_proxy` is set in the container environment, the `traefik healthcheck --ping` command fails with: ``` Bad healthcheck status: 400 Bad Request ``` This occurs because: 1. The healthcheck constructs a URL using `pingEntryPoint.GetAddress()`, which returns just the port (e.g., `:8080`) when the entrypoint address is configured as `:8080` 2. This results in a malformed URL: `http://:8080/ping` (no hostname) 3. Go's HTTP client automatically uses the `http_proxy` environment variable 4. Since the URL has no hostname, it cannot be matched against `no_proxy` settings 5. The request is sent through the proxy, which returns a 400 Bad Request for the invalid URL > Note: While the malformed URL (http://:8080/ping) was a contributing factor, the root cause was that healthcheck shouldn't use a proxy. ### More - [ ] Added/updated tests - [ ] Added/updated documentation ### Additional Notes
author: olamilekan000

commits.message:
fix health-check ping when http_proxy env is set

Signed-off-by: olalekan odukoya <odukoyaonline@gmail.com>

title: Add Scarf Analytics to documentation
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

Adds Scarf.sh analytics pixel to the documentation for anonymous, cookieless company-level traffic intelligence.

I evaluated three options:
- Template override (custom_dir) — Would require copying the theme's entire main.html and maintaining it separately. Rejected because the traefik-labs theme already has a complex main.html, and local overrides would break on theme updates.

- Adding to the theme repo (traefik/mkdocs-material) — The theme is open source and shared. Adding a Proxy-specific tracking ID there doesn't make sense since the theme itself isn't what gets deployed.

- JavaScript injection via extra.js — Adds the pixel through the existing script infrastructure. No theme modifications needed, survives theme upgrades, and keeps the tracking scoped to Proxy docs only.

Option 3 is the most maintainable solution.
author: sheddy-traefik

title: Fix code copy button positioning
body: ### What does this PR do? To enhance the documentation and usability ( UX), this PR fixes the visibility/placement of the “copy to clipboard” button on code blocks. This PR enables the theme’s built-in clipboard feature and adds a small CSS override so the injected button is anchored to the actual code block container. ### Motivation Issue #12498 reports that the docs don’t show a copy button on code snippets. The author of the issue requests for that feature so that UX would be greatly enhanced. Fixes #12498. ### More - [ ] Added/updated tests (not applicable for a small docs/theme change) - [x] Added/updated CSS ### Additional Notes Changes are intentionally minimal: - enable the built-in clipboard feature via `theme.features` - add a dedicated CSS file to anchor `button.md-clipboard` to `pre.highlight` - include it via `extra_css` in `docs/mkdocs.yml` - No custom JS was added. ### Tests ``` docker run --rm -v /Users/anurag/workspace/github/traefik-repo/traefik-docs/docs:/app traefik-docs-check /verify.sh === Checking HTML content... = Documentation checked successfully. ``` ### Use of AI - Used AI to explore the best way popular docs provide this feature. ### Screenshots

body:
### What does this PR do?

Adds missing RBAC configuration file and documentation for the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider.

### Motivation

Fixes #12415

Users encountered permission errors when using `watchNamespaceSelector`:
```
listing namespaces: namespaces is forbidden
```

The Ingress NGINX provider was missing RBAC documentation, unlike other Kubernetes providers (CRD, Gateway).

### More
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Follows the same pattern as `kubernetes-crd-rbac.yml` and `kubernetes-gateway-rbac.yml`.

-->

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
These certificate and related script were historically added in 71bb22fd0a120367ae249415cf8321104615fdb0 and 87e6285cf6a8418ed5e18b6e9cf0e24a80788b71 respectively, but have since gone unused as the `Dockerfile` here (and in the official image) now are based on Alpine with bundled certificates. Hence there is no longer any need to maintain this (outdated) state in this repository.

See also:
- https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/4854dee20872a2002374d7b08f4a2977b3cdaec1/Dockerfile#L4
- https://github.com/traefik/traefik-library-image/blob/521552748023808b53cc4cf3cbfec23274a8cda1/v3.6/alpine/Dockerfile#L2
- https://github.com/traefik/traefik-library-image/blob/521552748023808b53cc4cf3cbfec23274a8cda1/v3.6/scratch/Dockerfile#L2

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
When working on #12435 I found a reference to the 'unusual' `alpine:edge` docker tag, which subsequently lead me to find out it could be safely removed.

- [ ] Added/updated tests

title: Optimize GitHub Actions workflows
body:
This PR optimizes GitHub Actions workflows by adding appropriate timeouts and updating action versions:

## Changes
- Add timeouts to prevent hanging jobs:
  - Experimental build: 90 minutes
  - Release build: 120 minutes
  - Release job: 60 minutes
  - Integration build: 60 minutes
  - Integration test: 90 minutes
  - Conformance tests: 60 minutes
- Update `actions/checkout` from v4 to v5 in:
  - sync-docker-images workflow
  - Gateway API conformance workflow
  - Knative conformance workflow

## Motivation
The GitHub Actions workflows were missing timeout configurations, which could lead to jobs running indefinitely. Adding timeouts improves CI reliability and prevents resource waste. Updating action versions ensures we have the latest features and security fixes.

## Impact
- Improved CI reliability with timeout protections
- Better resource management on GitHub runners
- Up-to-date action dependencies for better security and features
author: luojiyin1987

- [ ] Added/updated tests

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

The current `Dockerfile` tags both here and on `v3` are quite restrictive in their respective NodeJS versions;
using a more loose tag is probably slightly neater.
A first quick glance seems to suggest everything is still fine on the latest LTS,
but I haven't extensively tested just yet.

If wanted I can create a dedicated MR for changes there.

title: Change ForwardAuth error log level from DEBUG to ERROR
body:
## What does this PR do?

When the ForwardAuth middleware fails to reach the authentication server, it returns a 500 error but logs at DEBUG level, making it difficult to diagnose issues in production without enabling debug logging.

This change promotes the error log to ERROR level for better observability.

## Motivation

As reported in #12234, when the ForwardAuth middleware cannot reach the auth server (e.g., DNS resolution failure, network issues), users receive 500 errors with no indication in logs unless DEBUG level is enabled. This makes troubleshooting production issues very difficult.

### Example log before this change:
```
2025-11-04T11:22:57Z DBG github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go:196 > Error calling <host>:8080/v0/auth:check error="..." 
```

### Example log after this change:
```
2025-11-04T11:22:57Z ERR github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go:196 > Error calling <host>:8080/v0/auth:check error="..."
```

## Changes

- Changed log level from `Debug()` to `Error()` in `pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go` line 198

Fixes #12234
author: murataslan1

reviews.body:
Thank you for your contribution @murataslan1.

As it's a bugfix, could you rebase the PR on the branch v3.6 to allow us to merge it please?

commits.message:
fix(middleware): change ForwardAuth error log level from DEBUG to ERROR

When the ForwardAuth middleware fails to reach the authentication server,
it returns a 500 error but logs at DEBUG level, making it difficult to
diagnose issues in production without enabling debug logging.

This change promotes the error log to ERROR level for better observability.

Fixes #12234

title: Bump github.com/quic-go/quic-go to v0.57.1
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

Update quic-go to fix the QUIC events with session tickets disabled on Go 1.26


### Motivation

> With https://go-review.googlesource.com/c/go/+/528755 (which will be part of Go 1.26), `tls.QUICConn.SendSessionTicket` now won't return an error when session tickets are disabled via `tls.Config`. Instead, it will just return a `tls.QUICNoEvent`. The current code panics when encountering this condition. Instead, we should just proceed (and not send a session ticket to the client).


https://github.com/quic-go/quic-go/pull/5462
https://github.com/quic-go/quic-go/releases/tag/v0.57.1

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->

title: Auto-negotiate Docker API Version
body:
### What does this PR do?

This PR fixes #12253 by removing the hardcoded docker api version and relying on auto-negotiation between the docker client (sdk) and server using [WithAPIVersionNegotiation()](https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/v28.5.2/client/options.go#L221).

### Motivation

Traefik currently fails to work with Docker v29 with the following errors:

```
2025-11-10T23:03:43Z ERR Failed to retrieve information of the docker client and server host error="Error response from daemon: client version 1.24 is too old. Minimum supported API version is 1.44, please upgrade your client to a newer version" providerName=docker
2025-11-10T23:03:43Z ERR Provider error, retrying in 2.356192979s error="Error response from daemon: client version 1.24 is too old. Minimum supported API version is 1.44, please upgrade your client to a newer version" providerName=docker
```

Fixes #12253

### Additional Notes

- Tested on production servers with Docker 29.0.0.
- Test docker image available with tag `felixbuenemann/traefik:v3.6` on docker hub (created with `make build-image`)
author: felixbuenemann

reviews.body:
thanks! A colleague actually noticed the hard-coded value in Traefik, and we were planning to open a PR, but looks like that dropped off our list 🙈 

Changes SGTM (with my limited knowledge of this codebase); I left some suggestions / thoughts (but those definitely could be handled separately).
FWIW, changes in this LGTM (assuming CI is happy)
Thanks for fixing this @felixbuenemann and for the review @thaJeztah 👍

title: Avoid allocations in readLoop by using sync.Pool
body: ### What does this PR do? Adds `sync.Pool` into `Listener` in order to avoid unneeded allocations in `readLoop` ### Motivation - Speed: sync-pool is ~6% faster - Throughput is ~6% higher - Memory allocation: ~44% fewer bytes/op ```bash $ benchstat old.txt sync-pool.txt goos: darwin goarch: arm64 pkg: github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/udp cpu: Apple M3 Max │ old.txt │ sync-pool.txt │ │ sec/op │ sec/op vs base │ ReadLoopAllocations 1.907µ ± 6% 1.796µ ± 10% -5.87% (p=0.012 n=20) │ old.txt │ sync-pool.txt │ │ B/s │ B/s vs base │ ReadLoopAllocations 4.000Gi ± 6% 4.249Gi ± 11% +6.22% (p=0.012 n=20) │ old.txt │ sync-pool.txt │ │ B/op │ B/op vs base │ ReadLoopAllocations 725.5 ± 5% 401.5 ± 6% -44.66% (p=0.000 n=20) │ old.txt │ sync-pool.txt │ │ allocs/op │ allocs/op vs base │ ReadLoopAllocations 0.000 ± 0% 0.000 ± 0% ~ (p=1.000 n=20) ¹ ¹ all samples are equal ``` I ran the benchmark with these flags ``` go test -run=^$ -bench=BenchmarkReadLoopAllocations -benchmem -cpu=1 -count=20 ``` ### More - [ ] Added/updated tests - [ ] Added/updated documentation ### Additional Notes Without sync.Pool With sync.Pool
author: arturmelanchyk

commits.message:
Use sync.Pool in Listener's readLoop

title: Allow publishing services with type ExternalName
body:
### What does this PR do?

This allows pointing `PublishedService` at an external hostname (such as an externally managed CNAME) for e.g. external-dns to pick up

### Motivation

I'd like to migrate my existing cluster to use cloudflare tunnels: to do so, we are replacing our existing `type: LoadBalancer` published service with one that has `type: ExternalName, externalName: mytunnel.cfargotunnel.com`

This seems to be currently supported in the `ingress-nginx` provider, but not the normal `ingress` one!

title: Add Traefik Hub demo in dashboard
body:
### What does this PR do? Adds a Traefik Hub demo to the WebUI dashboard to showcase Hub's API management capabilities. The demo displays Hub-specific content using a dynamically loaded remote web component. For security, the remote bundle is cryptographically signed, and signature verification must pass before the script executes. Verification runs in a Web Worker to isolate it from the main thread and prevent blocking the UI. Improvement is also made on the Page layout rendering, to avoid the side navigation bar from getting re-rendered every time user changing the path.
Preview Dashboard homepage: Hub demo:

title: Allow discovering non-running Docker containers
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation fixes or enhancements:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.0

Bug fixes:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.0

Enhancements:
- for Traefik v2: we only accept bug fixes
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.0

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

This PR implements the `allowEmptyServices` capabilities to the Docker provider by allowing non running containers to be discovered.


### Motivation

Non-running containers not returning 404, but 503 instead and having the full middleware chain built and executed on requests.

Fixes https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/9907

Fixes #7842

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Test it yourself:

```yaml
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:local
    command:
      - --entryPoints.http.address=:80
      - --providers.docker=true
      - --providers.docker.allowEmptyServices=true
      - --log.level=DEBUG
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
    volumes:
      - '/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock'

  whoami:
    image: containous/whoami:v1.5.0
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.whoami.rule=PathPrefix(`/whoami`)
```

1. Pull this PR
2. `make`
3. `docker build -t traefik:test .`
4. `docker compose up`
5. `docker compose down whoami`
6. `curl http://localhost:8080/whoami` -> 503

You can add any middleware, and they will get exectued, which is the most important part.
author: acouvreur

reviews.body:
Thanks @acouvreur for this contribution!
I just made a few suggestions. Other than that LGTM

Hi @acouvreur,

We pushed a review commit.
We limited the impact of the option only to the Docker provider, since we identified that the `allowEmptyService` Swarm provider option should be enough to support the Sablier use case.
However, currently the `allowEmptyService` option is not working as expected (and as you mentioned it, using the `LBSwarm` option is the current workaround). We opened an issue for that: #12196

Thank you!

commits.message:
feat(provider): allow non-running containers to be retrieved using allowEmptyServices

This will allow  middleware to be used on containers that are currently not running.

title: Add least time load balancing strategy
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.5

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.5

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

Adds a new `leasttime` load balancing strategy that selects backend servers based on average response time (TTFB) and active connection count, weighted by server capacity.

### Motivation

Traefik's existing strategies (WRR, P2C, HRW) don't consider response time/latency as a selection criterion.
The `leasttime` strategy routes traffic to the fastest-responding servers while maintaining fair load distribution.
It is particularly interesting when:
- Backend servers have heterogeneous performance (different hardware, locations, or load)
- Servers experience performance degradation (sustained load, network latency changes)
- Multi-datacenter deployments with varying RTT

### More

- [x] Added/updated documentation

title: Do not fail when pod is not found in K8sAttributesDetector
body:
### What does this PR do?

Fix tracing module startup failure in case of running with host network.


### Motivation

When running traefik with `hostNetwork: true` mode, pod inherits the hostname from the node it's running on. 
As a result, K8sAttributesDetector can't find a pod with that name, causing the entire tracing module to fail to start:

```
Unable to create tracer error="building resource: error detecting resource: 
getting pod metadata: pods \"<nodeName>\" not found"
```

Since it returns an empty resource instead of failing when [permissions are missing](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/v3.5.2/pkg/types/k8sdetector.go#L56), it seems we should do the same if the pod isn't found.

As an additional way to avoid failures, I've added retrieving the pod name from the `POD_NAME` environment variable.


### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Maybe update some docs around [here](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/v3.5.2/docs/content/migrate/v3.md?plain=1#L459), but I think it's not worth mentioning.

<details>
<summary>Current workaround</summary>


* Build `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` environment variable on your own:
```yaml
# ... pod manifest
    env:
      - name: POD_NAME
        valueFrom:
          fieldRef:
            fieldPath: metadata.name
      - name: POD_UID
        valueFrom:
          fieldRef:
            fieldPath: metadata.uid
      - name: POD_NAMESPACE
        valueFrom:
          fieldRef:
            fieldPath: metadata.namespace
      - name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
        value: >-
          k8s.pod.name=$(POD_NAME),
          k8s.pod.uid=$(POD_UID),
          k8s.namespace.name=$(POD_NAMESPACE)
```

* All the following actions of trying to get pod metadata will be [skipped](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/v3.5.2/pkg/types/k8sdetector.go#L25)
</details>
author: xe-leon

reviews.body:
Hello @xe-leon and thanks for this contribution,

We are fine with introducing the `IsNotFound` check to avoid blocking the user when the metadata cannot be determined because of a missing pod.

Instead of introducing another way to specify the [`POD_NAME`](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12096/files#diff-92ba44df202dd77a453dc1376739155e36b5d87b7994c1a4257e2f92503dc264R44), we would prefer to enhance the documentation (for example, in the reference documentation for metrics, tracing, and logs in the OTEL section) and keep only the standard way to declare attributes with the `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` env variable.

commits.message:
[tracing] Do not fail if pod not found; Try POD_NAME environment variable

Signed-off-by: Evgenii Domashenkin <410-xe-leon@users.noreply.mindbox.gitlab.yandexcloud.net>

move err check

Signed-off-by: Evgenii Domashenkin <410-xe-leon@users.noreply.mindbox.gitlab.yandexcloud.net>

revert POD_NAME from env

Signed-off-by: Evgenii Domashenkin <410-xe-leon@users.noreply.mindbox.gitlab.yandexcloud.net>

title: Implement HTTP2 HPACK table size options
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.5

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.5

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

Exposes HTTP2 HPACK options for the user to customise.

The default is zero, which tells Golang to use it's default of 4096 bytes.

Configuring a different header cache size allows the user to trade memory for the opportunity to cache more headers, reducing the amount of data sent over the wire. For very large headers this might have a big saving on network traffic, especially in network constrained scenarios.

Closes #12045

### Motivation

Motivation is detailed in the #12045 issue, but to summarise: being able to have a larger cache may help reduce network traffic on applications that use large headers, which may be advantageous for network constrained environments.

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [x] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

Right now I've set the default to 0, which tells Golang to use it's default. Happy to switch it out instead so we explicitly set the default to 4096 so it's clearer to the user what the default value actually being used is. I felt this was a bit of a tradeoff since if Go updates on their side, it's likely for a reason and will likely fit 99.99% of use cases.
author: GCHQDeveloper548

Hi @kianelbo! 👋

Thanks for your contribution! 

I think it would be valuable to enhance the documentation section with a bit more prominent guidance about the security implications of leaving maxBodySize unset. This could help users make more informed decisions about appropriate values for their specific use cases.

Would it be okay if I push a small documentation enhancement directly to your branch? I'd like to add a dedicated subsection that provides:
  - Clear security recommendations
  - Practical examples with specific byte values
  - Guidelines for different scenarios (web forms, APIs, etc.)

This would complement your work and make the security aspects even more visible to users. Let me know if you're comfortable with me contributing to your branch, or if you'd prefer to handle the documentation updates yourself.

Thanks again for the contribution!

I improved the documentation and the warning with a more actionable message.
LGTM
Thanks again for your contribution @kianelbo!

Hey @kianelbo,

Thank you for your contribution.
I just have a suggestion before approving the PR.

Thank you for this contribution @kianelbo 👍 

LGTM

In v3.5.3, the application version is no longer displayed next to the logo due to a regression in the version retrieval API call.
The API request to fetch the version information is not accounting for the `basePath` configuration value, causing the request to target an incorrect endpoint.

### Motivation

Fix a regression in v3.5.3 where the application version is not displayed next to the logo.

### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->
Fixes https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/12110

title: feat(provider/k8s/ingress-nginx): add limit-burst-multiplier annotation support
body:
### What does this PR do?
Adds support for the `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/limit-burst-multiplier` annotation in the Kubernetes Ingress-Nginx provider, extending the current basic RateLimit configuration to allow customizable burst token accumulation.

### Motivation
- The burst multiplier is a general setting that must apply to both RPM and RPS.
- NGINX core uses integer configurations; thus, it is kept as an `int` type to avoid decimal edge cases and precision problems.
- **Fallback mechanism via `getLimitBurstMultiplier`:** Instead of generating a complex new middleware flow, I introduced the `getLimitBurstMultiplier` helper function to compute the integer and neatly reuse it across both `applyLimitRPMConfiguration` and `applyLimitRPSConfiguration`. This helper also acts as a safeguard: if the parsed multiplier is `< 1` (e.g., negative or 0), it safely falls back to the Traefik token bucket default (`5`) to prevent accidental blocking of all traffic.

Follow-up to #12709 
Issue #12631 

### More
- [x] Added/updated unit tests (verified `Burst: 100` and `Burst: 50` fallback via `ingress-with-limit-burst-multiplier.yml` fixture)
- [x] Added/updated code generation & linting (`make generate fmt lint test-unit` passed)
author: amazon7737
