title: Display server weight in service detail view
body:
## What does this PR do?

Adds a **Weight** column to the Servers section in HTTP and TCP service detail pages in the dashboard.

## Motivation

As reported in #12192, the dashboard UI does not display the weight of servers within a service. This information is useful for users who configure weighted load balancing.

## Changes

### Files Modified:
- `webui/src/hooks/use-resource-detail.tsx` - Added `weight` to server type definition
- `webui/src/pages/http/HttpService.tsx` - Added Weight column to HTTP Servers grid
- `webui/src/pages/tcp/TcpService.tsx` - Added Weight column to TCP Servers grid

### Screenshots

The Servers section now displays:
| Status | URL/Address | Weight |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| UP/DOWN | server-url | 1 (default) or configured value |

## Notes
- Weight defaults to `1` when not explicitly configured (matching Traefik's default behavior)
- Updated grid layout to accommodate the new column

Fixes #12192
author: murataslan1

reviews.body:
Hello, thank you for opening the PR!

I left some suggestions as points of improvement. Additionally, if you could update the related test suites to take into account the newly added server weight, that'd be great :)

Hi @murataslan1,

Thanks for your contribution.

Sorry for the late answer.

Could you check why the UI unit tests are failing

## Pull request overview This PR adds a Weight column to the Servers section of both HTTP and TCP service detail pages in the Traefik dashboard, addressing issue #12192. The implementation refactors server status handling from object-based to array-based for better code organization and includes comprehensive test coverage for the new feature. **Key changes:** - Refactored server status handling from `ServerStatus` object to `ServerInfo` array using `useMemo` for both HTTP and TCP services - Added Weight column to server grids with default value of 1 when not explicitly configured - Updated grid layouts to accommodate the new column (3-column layout for HTTP with Status/URL/Weight, 3-column layout for TCP with Status/Address/Weight) ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | webui/src/hooks/use-resource-detail.tsx | Added `weight` property to server type definition in loadBalancer | | webui/src/pages/http/HttpService.tsx | Refactored server handling to array-based approach and added Weight column to Servers grid | | webui/src/pages/tcp/TcpService.tsx | Refactored server handling to array-based approach with null checks and added Weight column to Servers grid | | webui/src/pages/http/HttpService.spec.tsx | Added test coverage for server weight display including default and custom values | | webui/src/pages/tcp/TcpService.spec.tsx | Added test coverage for server weight display and updated existing test with servers array |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

- Added Weight column to HTTP Servers grid in HttpService.tsx
- Added Weight column to TCP Servers grid in TcpService.tsx
- Refactored server list building to use useMemo with map for cleaner code

Weight defaults to 1 when not explicitly configured, matching Traefik's

title: Support NGINX global auth annotation
body:
### What does this PR do?
  Implements `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/enable-global-auth` annotation                                                    
  for the Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider.                                                                                
                                                                                                                            
  When `GlobalAuthURL` is configured in the provider, it is applied to all                                                  
  Ingresses by default. Setting `enable-global-auth: "false"` on a specific
  Ingress disables the global auth for that Ingress only. A per-Ingress                                                     
  `auth-url` annotation always takes precedence over the global URL.                                                        
                                                                                                                            
  ### Motivation                                                                                                            
  Part of #12631.                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                            
  ### More
  - [x] Added/updated tests                                                                                                 
  - [x] Added/updated documentation

  ### Additional Notes
  The behavior mirrors the original ingress-nginx implementation:
  https://github.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/blob/main/internal/ingress/controller/template/template.go#L577
author: foxcool

commits.message: feat: implement nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/enable-global-auth annotation and globalAuthURL provider configuration support
test: add fixtures and tests for provider globalAuthURL parameter, enable-global-auth and auth-url anotations

title: Fix docker-compose.yaml location in traefik/setup/docker/
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

—>

**N.B:** Trying to follow PR guidelines to my best ability, have used branch v3.6 (Doc fix)

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. —>

When following guidance from the Docker [’setup’](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/setup/docker/) article, I came across a discrepancy. The `docker-compose.yaml` file provided directs a volume mount for `./dynamic` (in order to discover the `tls.yaml` file), however the docs instruct the selfsame docker file to be created within the folder `./dynamic` itself (from project root.

In terms of the tutorial itself, this doesn’t actually seem to break any functionality, however it does introduce an file watcher error message in docker logs:

```log
❯ docker-compose up
WARN[0000] a network with name proxy exists but was not created for project "dynamic".
Set `external: true` to use an existing network
[+] Running 2/2
 ✔ Container traefik  Created                                                                                                                             0.1s
 ✔ Container whoami   Created                                                                                                                             0.1s
Attaching to traefik, whoami
whoami  | 2026/03/21 13:55:45 Starting up on port 80
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z WRN Traefik can reject some encoded characters in the request path. When your backend is not fully compliant with [RFC 3986](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3986), it is recommended to set these options to `false` to avoid split-view situation. Refer to the documentation for more details: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/v3.6/migrate/v3/#encoded-characters-configuration-default-values
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Traefik version 3.6.11 built on 2026-03-19T09:18:31Z version=3.6.11
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Version check is enabled.
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Traefik checks for new releases to notify you if your version is out of date.
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF It also collects usage data during this process.
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Check the documentation to get more info: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/data-collection/
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF
traefik  | Stats collection is disabled.
traefik  | Help us improve Traefik by turning this feature on :)
traefik  | More details on: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/data-collection/
traefik  |
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Starting provider aggregator *aggregator.ProviderAggregator
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Starting provider *file.Provider
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z ERR Cannot start the provider *file.Provider error="error adding file watcher: no such file or directory"
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Starting provider *traefik.Provider
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Starting provider *docker.Provider
traefik  | 2026-03-21T13:55:45Z INF Starting provider *acme.ChallengeTLSALPN
```

When the `docker-compose.yaml` file is in the parent to `dynamic/`, this error doesn’t exist (and a seperate `certs/` dir need not be created within `dynamic/` as well)

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? —>

To assist with the clarity of Traefik docs

### More

- [ ] Added/updated tests
- [*] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->
author: ScottA38

reviews.body: Thanks for your contribution @ScottA38, I can understand the confusion and I think your PR is valid. Please rebase the branch to be up to date.

title: Fix comment and unnecessary allocation in withRoutingPath
body:
### What does this PR do?

Two small fixes in `withRoutingPath`:

- Use `WriteByte` instead of `WriteString(string(byte))` to avoid a per-character string allocation
- Fix comment that says "non-allowed characters" when it means "non-reserved characters" — the function decodes characters that are *not* in the reserved set, which is the opposite of "non-allowed"

Noticed while working on #12666.

### Motivation

Cleaner code in a hot path.

title: Fix TLS behavior in ingress-nginx provider
body:
<!--
PLEASE READ THIS MESSAGE.

Documentation:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Bug:
- for Traefik v2: use branch v2.11 (security fixes only)
- for Traefik v3: use branch v3.6

Enhancements:
- use branch master

HOW TO WRITE A GOOD PULL REQUEST? https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/submitting-pull-requests/

-->

### What does this PR do?

<!-- A brief description of the change being made with this pull request. -->
This PR fixes the TLS behavior in the ingress-nginx provider to match the original behavior:
- If an ingress doesn't have a TLS section, default certificate will be provided but redirect will not be forced.
- If an ingress has an invalid certificate, it will fallback to default certificate.

### Motivation

<!-- What inspired you to submit this pull request? -->
- Fixes https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/12820
- Fixes https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/12748

### More

- [x] Added/updated tests
- [ ] Added/updated documentation

### Additional Notes

<!-- Anything else we should know when reviewing? -->

Co-authored-by: Gina A. <70909035+gndz07@users.noreply.github.com>

title: Support ResponseHeaderModifier filter
body:
### What does this PR do?

This pull request introduces the support of the ResponseHeaderModifier filter as defined in the Gateway API [specification](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/reference/spec/#gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1.HTTPRouteFilter.

### Motivation

To enhance the Gateway API specification support.

Here is the updated conformance report:

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1alpha1
date: "2024-08-08T16:04:41+02:00"
gatewayAPIChannel: experimental
gatewayAPIVersion: v1.1.0
implementation:
  contact:
  - '@traefik/maintainers'
  organization: traefik
  project: traefik
  url: https://traefik.io/
  version: dev
kind: ConformanceReport
mode: default
profiles:
- core:
    result: success
    statistics:
      Failed: 0
      Passed: 33
      Skipped: 0
  extended:
    result: success
    statistics:
      Failed: 0
      Passed: 10
      Skipped: 0
    supportedFeatures:
    - GatewayPort8080
    - HTTPRouteHostRewrite
    - HTTPRouteMethodMatching
    - HTTPRoutePathRedirect
    - HTTPRoutePathRewrite
    - HTTPRoutePortRedirect
    - HTTPRouteQueryParamMatching
    - HTTPRouteResponseHeaderModification
    - HTTPRouteSchemeRedirect
    unsupportedFeatures:
    - GatewayHTTPListenerIsolation
    - GatewayStaticAddresses
    - HTTPRouteBackendRequestHeaderModification
    - HTTPRouteBackendTimeout
    - HTTPRouteParentRefPort
    - HTTPRouteRequestMirror
    - HTTPRouteRequestMultipleMirrors
    - HTTPRouteRequestTimeout
  name: GATEWAY-HTTP
  summary: Core tests succeeded. Extended tests succeeded.
```
